title: FMD - Disconnecting/Connecting earbuds to see how it is displayed on the list and map
scenario: Find My Device list and map in Poly Lens Menu does not correctly update earbud and charge case connection status after disconnecting and reconnecting individual earbuds
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure both earbuds are connected to the device.
3. Tap the Poly Lens Menu icon (Find My Device).
4. Verify the Find My Device list and map display both earbuds and the charge case with appropriate connection status tags (based on device type: Osprey, Tern/Emu, or Flamingo).
5. Disconnect the right earbud only (e.g., place it in the charge case and close the lid, or turn it off via device settings).
6. Return to the Find My Device list and map. Observe the display for the right earbud, left earbud, and charge case.
7. Disconnect the left earbud (both earbuds now disconnected).
8. Return to the Find My Device list and map. Observe the display for both earbuds and the charge case.
9. Reconnect the right earbud (e.g., remove it from the charge case or power it on).
10. Return to the Find My Device list and map. Observe the display for the right earbud, left earbud, and charge case.
11. Reconnect the left earbud (both earbuds now connected).
12. Return to the Find My Device list and map. Observe the final display for both earbuds and the charge case.
expected: The Find My Device list and map should correctly reflect the connection status of each earbud and the charge case at every step. Behavior varies by device type: Osprey shows 3 parts (left earbud, right earbud, charge case) with individual connection tags; Tern/Emu shows 2 parts (left earbud, right earbud) with connection tags; Flamingo shows 3 parts (left earbud, right earbud, charge case) with connection tags. When an earbud is disconnected, its tag should update to 'Disconnected' or 'Offline'. When reconnected, the tag should return to 'Connected'. The charge case status should remain accurate throughout.
